name: Chocolate-Apricot Passover Torte
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line the bottoms of two 9-inch round baking pans with parchment paper or wax paper.
2. In a medium saucepan over low heat, melt butter. Add 1-1/2 cups sugar and cocoa; stir until well blended. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
3. In a large bowl, beat egg yolks until slightly thickened. Gradually add the cocoa mixture, beating until blended. Stir in water and vanilla. Mix ground almonds and cake meal together; fold about half of this mixture into the chocolate batter. In a small bowl, beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually add the remaining 1-1/2 cups sugar, beating until stiff peaks form. Gently fold the remaining almond mixture into the beaten whites. Carefully pour the egg white mixture into the chocolate batter, folding gently until well combined. Pour the batter into the prepared pans.
4. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Let cool for 10 minutes (the cake will settle slightly). Remove from pans and place on wire racks to cool completely.
5. Place one layer on a serving plate. Heat apricot preserves; strain. Discard the fruit. Spread the melted preserves over the top of the first layer. Top with the second layer.
6. Prepare CHOCOLATE CREAM FROSTING as directed. Spread over the top and sides of the torte. Refrigerate until serving time. Garnish with whole almonds, if desired. Cover; refrigerate leftover torte. 12 servings.
CHOCOLATE CREAM FROSTING
In a medium bowl, stir together 1/2 cup sugar and 1/4 cup Hershey's Cocoa. Add 1 cup (1/2 pint) whipping cream and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract. Beat until stiff. About 2 cups frosting.
